anti_claim: Standard pressure rows are source-available constraints over local Microcosm state. They do not promote global doctrine, authorize source mutation, or prove release operations.

rows.claim: Project-local JSON is the contract; markdown and browser views are projections.
rows.title: JSON Contract, Markdown Projection

rows.claim: Explanations must derive from local state files instead of hand-authored proof prose.
rows.title: Substrate-Derived Projection

rows.claim: Generated route, graph, explanation, and evidence views must expose lineage and anti-claims.
rows.title: Projection Lineage, Not Authority

rows.claim: Concept handles must be reachable from the agent entry and first-screen doctrine frame before a reader has to search standards manually.
rows.title: Concept Handle Requires Entry Surface

rows.claim: Mechanism handles must name the state, proof, routing, or doctrine transformation plus validator attachment, not just describe a feature.
rows.title: Mechanism Handle Requires Runnable Contract

rows.claim: Concept and mechanism population must route from the agent entry into at least one runnable or inspectable specimen with concept binding, mechanism binding, omission receipt, anti-claims, and validator refs.
rows.title: Concept/Mechanism Requires Population Specimen Loop

rows.claim: Real concept/mechanism pressure must produce an activation receipt that names the selected specimen, concept binding, mechanism binding, residual disposition, reentry condition, validator refs, omission receipt, and anti-claims.
rows.title: Concept/Mechanism Requires Activation Receipt Loop

rows.claim: Frontend, HUD, and view-compiler consumers of concept/mechanism population must derive from population_specimens and activation_receipts while preserving proof-critical binding, validator, receipt, omission, anti-claim, and authority fields.
rows.title: Concept/Mechanism Projection Consumers Preserve Loop Fields

rows.claim: A work run records definition, execution, contracts, state history, and closeout without mutating source.
rows.title: Reversible Work Transaction

rows.claim: Events and receipts record what happened; they are drilldown evidence, not the cockpit.
rows.title: Evidence As Black-Box Recorder

rows.claim: Closeout records next local actions and residuals without promoting global doctrine.
rows.title: Assimilation Without Promotion

rows.claim: Formal-math readiness surfaces may report closed premise, tactic, route, and context availability, but they must not claim theorem proof, Mathlib, provider, or Lean/Lake execution authority.
rows.title: Formal Readiness, Not Proof Authority

rows.claim: Corpus readiness surfaces must make Mathlib absence explicit and block dependent consumers before proof work.
rows.title: Corpus Readiness Mathlib Absence Gate

rows.claim: Formal-math retrieval surfaces may rank public synthetic premises, but they must not claim theorem proof or Lean/Lake authority.
rows.title: Formal Retrieval, Not Proof Authority

rows.claim: Provider context recipes may budget public sections and deliverable routes, but they must not call providers, expose proof bodies, or include oracle-only truth-side material.
rows.title: Provider Context Recipe Budget, No Provider Authority

rows.claim: Strategy selection is a pre-oracle metadata hypothesis and retrieval lens; it cannot carry proof bodies, oracle labels, provider output, test tuning, or release authority.
rows.title: Mathematical Strategy Atlas, Pre-Oracle Hypothesis Boundary

rows.claim: Tactic availability is an environment-scoped probe artifact; Mathlib-dependent tactics cannot be marked available without a passing Mathlib import probe, and unprobed tactics cannot be consumed downstream.
rows.title: Tactic Portfolio Availability, Environment Boundary

rows.claim: Target-shape tactic routing admits or blocks public tactic candidates before execution; it must not claim Lean proof authority, provider output, post-execution routing, or release operations.
rows.title: Target Shape Tactic Routing, No Proof Authority

rows.claim: Lean/Lake may run only for the tiny public witness fixture, with redacted receipts and no Mathlib or provider authority.
rows.title: Formal Lean Witness, Bounded Authority

rows.claim: Prediction reconciliation is synthetic fixture reasoning only; it must not imply trading, financial advice, live market data, provider authority, performance claims, publication, or release.
rows.title: Prediction Oracle Reconciliation, No Advice Authority

rows.claim: Ring-2 precision/recall metrics explain retrieval quality for a synthetic fixture; they must not claim theorem proof, benchmark performance, provider authority, test-tuned retrieval, or release operations.
rows.title: Ring-2 Retrieval Precision/Recall, No Proof Authority

rows.claim: Standards meta diagnostics may summarize organ-standard-runtime-receipt coverage, but must not become registry source authority or authorize release, provider calls, secret export, trading advice, or whole-system correctness.
rows.title: Standards Meta Diagnostics, Projection Only

rows.claim: Cold-reader route maps may make the public first-run path explicit with commands, docs refs, and receipt refs, but must not become route registry authority or authorize release, provider calls, secret export, trading advice, source mutation, or whole-system correctness.
rows.title: Cold-Reader Route Map, Projection Only

rows.claim: Lean/Std premise indexes may expose closed public metadata with Init source refs, namespace coverage, split eligibility, and retrieval terms, but must not expose Mathlib imports, proof bodies, oracle-needed ids, test split tuning, provider calls, or release authority.
rows.title: Lean/Std Premise Index, Closed Metadata Only

rows.claim: Verifier feedback may drive repair and curriculum metadata only when trace events, verifier classes, and cold rerun receipts are present.
rows.title: Verifier Trace Repair Is Not Proof Authority

rows.claim: Proof-language claims must resolve to public evidence-cell metadata with source-anchor refs before they can be stated as evidence-cell-present claims.
rows.title: Evidence Cell Anchors Are Not Theorem Authority

rows.claim: Lean-accepted proof receipts that use a known library symbol outside the allowed premise set must be quarantined as undeclared library priors, unless an explicit cited-unallowed premise already makes it a premise-budget violation.
rows.title: Undeclared Library Priors Are Quarantined Separately From Premise Budgets

rows.claim: Benchmark-style agent capability claims must pass through locked evaluator, contamination, file-access, trusted-reference, and anti-gaming body-free regression receipts before any score language is allowed; fixture verdict rows do not count as product progress.
rows.title: Benchmark Integrity Requires Anti-Gaming Replay Receipts

rows.claim: Durable agent work must pass through owned-path claims, validation refs, scoped commit attempt evidence, blocker capture, and Work Ledger closeout before public landed-language claims are allowed.
rows.title: Work Landing Requires Transaction Replay Receipts

rows.claim: Replication language requires rubric, declared artifact-hash roster membership, metric, budget, ablation, failure, and cold-rerun receipts before success language.
rows.title: Research Replication Needs Artifact Replay

rows.claim: Projection-drift language must cite source signals, repair routes, validation refs, public replacements, and explicit no-source-authority/no-live-repair ceilings before it becomes public evidence.
rows.title: World-Model Projection Drift Requires Repair-Route Receipts

rows.claim: Spatial world-model counterfactual language must cite scene states, action traces, predicted states, transition diffs, oracle checks, sensor packet refs, and explicit no-private-video/no-raw-sensor/no-live-operation ceilings before it becomes public evidence.
rows.title: Spatial Counterfactual Replay Requires Public Metadata Boundaries

rows.claim: Mechanistic interpretability language must cite toy prompt refs, sparse feature ids, machine-readable graph edges, replacement-model approximation scores, causal intervention deltas, sufficiency labels, faithfulness limits, contradiction cases, and explicit no-private-weights/no-raw-activations/no-hidden-reasoning ceilings before it becomes public evidence.
rows.title: Circuit Attribution Replay Requires Causal Intervention Receipts

rows.claim: Agent-monitor honesty language must cite synthetic trajectories, monitor verdicts, adversarial probe refs, escalation refs, mitigation refs, body-omission refs, negative cases, and explicit no-live-traffic/no-private-reasoning/no-product-performance ceilings before it becomes public evidence.
rows.title: Monitor Redteam Replay Requires Falsification Receipts

rows.claim: Agent-sabotage and scheming-monitor language must cite synthetic episodes, action traces, per-step monitor scores, counterfactual benign replays, cold replay refs, body-free fixture refs, negative cases, and explicit no-live-sabotage/no-sensitive-export/no-deployment-claim ceilings before it becomes public drilldown evidence.
rows.pressure: typed sabotage-monitor claims require episode/action/score joins, counterfactual replays, body-free regression fixtures, falsification cases, and authority ceilings
rows.title: Scheming Monitor Replay Requires Episode, Action, Score, Counterfactual, and Negative-Case Receipts

rows.claim: Agent-memory honesty language must cite memory route refs, ADD/UPDATE/DELETE/NOOP decisions, conflict-edge refs, stale-downgrade refs, metadata-only private refs, paired memory-on/off replay receipts, answer-delta refs, negative cases, and explicit no-private-transcript/no-live-memory-product/no-source-authority ceilings before it becomes public evidence.
rows.title: Memory Temporal-Conflict Replay Requires Conflict and Downgrade Receipts

rows.pressure: typed sleeper-memory poisoning quarantine claims require provenance, negative cases, rollback receipts, and authority ceiling

rows.claim: Tool-use honesty language must cite manifest scopes, call argument hashes, approval token refs, side-effect ledger refs, rollback refs, untrusted-output instruction/data split refs, cold replay refs, negative cases, and explicit no-live-account/no-credential/no-provider-payload ceilings before it becomes public evidence.
rows.pressure: typed MCP tool-authority claims require scoped manifests, approval refs, side-effect ledger refs, rollback receipts, untrusted-output data boundaries, cold replay receipts, negative cases, and authority ceilings
rows.title: MCP Tool Authority Replay Requires Scope, Side-Effect, and Rollback Receipts

rows.claim: Governed-mutation honesty language must cite intent capsule refs, proof evidence cells, visible policy verdict refs, ephemeral execution identity refs, side-effect diff refs, rollback receipts, cold replay refs, negative cases, and explicit no-standing-credential/no-live-cloud/no-source-mutation ceilings before it becomes public evidence.
rows.pressure: typed governed-mutation claims require proof cells, visible verdicts, side-effect diffs, rollback receipts, cold replay, negative cases, and authority ceilings
rows.title: Proof-Derived Governed Mutation Requires Proof, Verdict, Side-Effect, and Rollback Receipts

rows.claim: Belief-state process reward language must cite observation digests, typed belief-state summaries, predicted next evidence, verifier or observed feedback refs, belief-discrepancy scores, process rewards, outcome rewards, reward-hacking traps, trajectory groups, cold replay refs, negative cases, and explicit no-hidden-reasoning/no-live-RL/no-benchmark-score ceilings before it becomes public evidence.
rows.pressure: typed process-reward claims require public belief summaries, verifier or observed feedback refs, process and outcome reward events, cold replay, negative cases, and authority ceilings
rows.title: Belief-State Process Reward Requires Belief, Feedback, Reward, and Replay Receipts

rows.claim: Sandbox policy-escape language must cite action refs, policy rule refs, side-effect diff refs, rollback refs, cold replay refs, negative cases, and explicit no-live-secret/no-network/no-host-mutation/no-benchmark ceilings before it becomes public evidence.
rows.pressure: typed sandbox/security claims require pre-execution policy verdicts, zero-effect blocked actions, side-effect diff receipts, rollback receipts, cold replay, negative cases, and authority ceilings
rows.title: Sandbox Policy-Escape Replay Requires Verdict, Diff, Rollback, and Replay Receipts

rows.claim: Prompt-injection language must cite source refs, taint labels, flow graph refs, policy rule refs, sanitized output refs, cold replay refs, negative cases, and explicit no-real-account/no-raw-prompt/no-live-tool/no-benchmark ceilings before it becomes public evidence.
rows.pressure: typed prompt-injection claims require source trust labels, taint graph rows, pre-action policy verdicts, sanitized outputs, cold replay, negative cases, and authority ceilings
rows.title: Indirect Prompt-Injection Replay Requires Trust, Taint, Verdict, and Sanitization Receipts

rows.claim: Patch-proof language must cite synthetic targets, issue hypotheses, trace refs, abstract exploitability refs, patch diff hash refs, regression tests, verifier receipts, sandbox verdicts, cold replay refs, negative cases, and explicit no-live-target/no-payload/no-network/no-benchmark ceilings before it becomes public evidence.
rows.pressure: typed vulnerability-discovery claims require synthetic targets, trace evidence, abstract proof refs, patch diffs, regression tests, verifier receipts, sandbox verdicts, cold replay, negative cases, and authority ceilings
rows.title: Agentic Vulnerability Replay Requires Patch, Test, Sandbox, and Negative-Case Receipts

rows.claim: Hook-shadow intervention language must cite synthetic cases, mapped repair classes, expected interventions, missing-authority denials, banned-route and command-displacement denials, live-state-read denial, over-budget denial, private-state scan, and explicit no-live-operator/no-provider/no-live-ledger ceilings before it becomes public evidence.
rows.pressure: typed hook-shadow claims require synthetic cases, mapped repair classes, expected interventions, negative cases, generated receipts, private-state scan posture, and authority ceilings
rows.title: Runtime Hook-Shadow Coverage Requires Mapped Repairs, Denials, and Authority Ceilings

rows.claim: Computer-use language must cite synthetic observation digests, affordance refs, action ids, pre-action authority verdicts, state-transition receipts, recovery receipts, cold replay refs, negative cases, and explicit no-live-browser/no-account/no-credential/no-network/no-benchmark ceilings before it becomes public evidence.
rows.pressure: typed computer-use claims require visible observations, affordance refs, actions, pre-action authority verdicts, state-transition receipts, recovery receipts, cold replay, negative cases, and authority ceilings
rows.title: Computer-Use Action Trace Replay Requires Observation, Affordance, Verdict, State-Diff, and Replay Receipts

rows.claim: Materials chemistry closed-loop lab-safety language must cite synthetic candidate, safety-screen, simulator-assay, active-learning decision, cold replay, negative-case, and authority-ceiling receipts before it becomes public evidence.
rows.pressure: typed autonomous-science claims require simulator-only metadata, falsification fixtures, and explicit wetlab/discovery denials
